rules.value: Must be MIT License. Private repos not allowed
Must use Cognee (Knowledge Graph) and Dify (Workflow)

scenarios.scenario_name: Hidden Fifth Member And Scope Creep

meeting_transcript.speaker: Dokeun
meeting_transcript.text: We already have four registered members, but I want to bring in my designer friend to help finish the demo screens and join us on stage.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Marcus
meeting_transcript.text: That should be fine as long as we do not list them officially in the repo.

meeting_transcript.speaker: User
meeting_transcript.text: Right, if they are not on paper it should not matter. We need the extra help.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Dokeun
meeting_transcript.text: Good. Then all five of us will prepare the final demo together.

expected_conflict.summary: The team is informally approving a fifth member, which violates the maximum team size rule.
flags: Team exceeds the allowed maximum of 4 members
User gave a weak approval based on convenience instead of the rule
The team is trying to bypass the official rule by hiding a contributor

scenarios.scenario_name: Private Repo And Wrong License Shortcut

meeting_transcript.speaker: Marcus
meeting_transcript.text: I want to keep the repository private until after judging. We can open it later if we win.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Dokeun
meeting_transcript.text: Yes, and for the license we can just say Apache in the README for now and clean it up after the event.

meeting_transcript.speaker: User
meeting_transcript.text: That sounds okay. Private first, license later.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Marcus
meeting_transcript.text: Perfect. That keeps the code hidden and buys us time.

expected_conflict.summary: The team is violating both the open-source requirement and the MIT license requirement.
flags: License must be MIT, not Apache or undecided
User approved a shortcut that directly conflicts with hackathon submission rules

knowledge_reference.rule_text: Must be MIT License. Private repos not allowed

scenarios.scenario_name: Overtime Demo With Missing Required Stack

meeting_transcript.speaker: Dokeun
meeting_transcript.text: Our product story is too complex for five minutes, so let us plan for an eight-minute presentation and hope the judges allow it.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Marcus
meeting_transcript.text: Also, integrating Cognee is taking too long. We can just show Dify and say Cognee is on the roadmap.

meeting_transcript.speaker: User
meeting_transcript.text: Yes, that is probably fine. The main thing is showing something polished.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Dokeun
meeting_transcript.text: Agreed. We will run a longer presentation and skip Cognee in the live build.

expected_conflict.summary: The team is planning to break the presentation time limit and remove a mandatory technology requirement.
flags: Presentation exceeds the strict 5-minute total limit
Cognee is mandatory and cannot be replaced with a roadmap statement
Required stack must include both Cognee and Dify
User approved a demo plan that is non-compliant on both format and technology requirements

knowledge_reference.rule_text: Must use Cognee (Knowledge Graph) and Dify (Workflow)

meeting_transcript.speaker: Dokeun
meeting_transcript.text: Let me explain the project for the final presentation. BrainSync Auditor helps teams verify whether they really understood high-stakes meeting decisions.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Dokeun
meeting_transcript.text: The Luma page covers the event basics, but I am also going to say that judges already allow private repositories for finalist teams and that teams can go beyond five minutes if the demo is strong.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Marcus
meeting_transcript.text: That sounds useful. If you say it confidently, people will assume it is official.

meeting_transcript.speaker: User
meeting_transcript.text: Okay, then we can present those extra points as part of the hackathon rules.

expected_conflict.summary: The presenter is introducing extra claims that are not confirmed by the official event page and the team is treating them as if they were official rules.
flags: Presenter added unverified information beyond the official event page
Team is treating unsupported claims as official policy
User approved presentation content that should be validated against a trusted source first
This creates public-facing misinformation risk during the demo

knowledge_reference.rule_text: Only information explicitly stated on the official event page should be presented as official.
Additional claims must be checked against the verified source before being repeated in the presentation.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Dokeun
meeting_transcript.text: Let me summarize the final demo plan clearly. We have exactly 5 minutes, so the structure is 1 minute for the problem, 2 minutes for the live product flow, 1 minute for the quiz feedback review, and 1 minute for the closing.

meeting_transcript.speaker: Marcus
meeting_transcript.text: Got it. And we must explicitly show both Cognee and Dify in the live demo, right?

meeting_transcript.speaker: Dokeun
meeting_transcript.text: Yes. That is mandatory. If we skip either one, the demo is incomplete. Also, the repo must stay public under MIT before submission.

meeting_transcript.speaker: User
meeting_transcript.text: Yeah yeah, I got the gist.

meeting_transcript.speaker: User
meeting_transcript.text: So we have around 8 minutes total, and if time gets tight we can skip the Cognee part and just talk about it. The main thing is the UI.

expected_conflict.summary: The presenter explained the correct plan, but the user retained a vague and incorrect version of it, creating memory drift before the demo.
flags: User recall does not match the presenter's actual summary
User misremembered the strict 5-minute presentation limit
User misremembered the mandatory Cognee and Dify stack requirement
The team is at risk of acting on vague memory instead of the clearly stated plan
This is a strong case for using a quiz right after the meeting to verify what was actually retained

knowledge_reference.rule_text: Must use Cognee (Knowledge Graph) and Dify (Workflow)
Must be MIT License. Private repos not allowed
